chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يَؤُمُّ النِّسَاءَ وَ الْمَرْأَةِ تَؤُمُّ النِّسَاءَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 52 – The man leading the women, and the woman leading the women

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/263)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/263)
text: الرَّجُلِ يَؤُمُّ النِّسَاءَ لَيْسَ مَعَهُنَّ رَجُلٌ فِي الْفَرِيضَةِ قَالَ نَعَمْ وَ إِنْ كَانَ مَعَهُ صَبِيٌّ فَلْيَقُمْ إِلَى جَانِبِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad, from Al Husayn, from Fazalat, from Hammad Bin Usman, from Ibrahim Bin Manmoun,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding the man who led the women (in Salāt), there not being (another) man with them, in the Obligatory (Salāt)’. Heasws said: ‘And even if there was a male child with him, so let him stand to his side’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ahmad ibn al-Husayn has narrated from Fadalah from Hammad ibn ‘Uthaman from Ibrahim ibn Maymun who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who leads women in obligatory Salah (prayer) where there are no other men. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can do so and if there is a child he stands up on his side.’”
